'''Graeme Horne''' (born 22 March 1985) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He retired at the end of the 2020 season.

He previously played for [[Hull FC]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

==Background==
Horne was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[Humberside]], England.

He is the younger brother of former [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international [[Richard Horne]].

==Playing career==
===Club career===
====Hull FC====
Horne signed for [[Hull F.C.]] when he was 17 years old, making his [[Super League]] début in 2003. He missed out on the matchday squad for their [[2005 Challenge Cup]] Final victory, but did play at [[Wembley Stadium]] in the Challenge Cup Final against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in 2008.

Hull reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], and Horne played from the interchange bench in his side's 4-26 loss.<ref>{{cite news|title=St Helens 26-4 Hull FC|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/6035553.stm|access-date=18 August 2013|newspaper=[[BBC News]]|date=14 October 2006}}</ref>

====Huddersfield Giants====
He left Hull in 2010 to join [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], playing for two seasons before returning to the city of Hull to join [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

====Hull KR====
Horne joined Hull KR from the 2012 season.

====York City Knights====
In November 2017 Horne signed a one-year deal with York having previously played for the club on dual registration.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/15670291.York_City_Knights_boosted_by_signing_of_Graeme_Horne_from_Hull_KR/ |title=York City Knights boosted by signing of Graeme Horne from Hull KR |work=[[The Press (York)|The Press]] |date=19 November 2017 |access-date=1 December 2017}}</ref>

====Doncaster====
Horne joined Doncaster in December 2019 to be coached by his brother [[Richard Horne|Richard]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/56124/graeme-horne-joins-brother-richard-at-doncaster|title=GRAEME HORNE JOINS BROTHER RICHARD AT DONCASTER|access-date=8 Dec 2020|website=www.rugby-league.com}}</ref> making his debut on 23 Feb 2020 in the Challenge Cup defeat 12-22 to [[Workington Town]]; playing at {{rlp|lf}}.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/competitions/challenge-cup-2020/game-4/workington-town-vs-doncaster/summary.html|title=Doncaster 12-22 Workington Town|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=8 Dec 2020}}</ref>

==International career==
=== England ===
Horne played two games for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 2006.
